content.problemStatement:
Debug the code in the IDE to solve the problem.

The code is supposed to do the following:


Take a positive integer as input.
If its between 1 - 7, prints the corresponding day of the week.
Else print, **Invalid input**. Check the sample test case.
constraints: N is an integer (any value, only 1‑7 are considered valid)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the corresponding day of the week (Monday for 1, Tuesday for 2, …, Sunday for 7). If N is not in the range 1‑7, print "Invalid input". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, N=5 maps to the 5th day which is Friday.
T=1, N=8 is outside 1‑7, so the program prints "Invalid input".

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Invalid input
Invalid input
Invalid input

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Tuesday
Invalid input
Monday
Sunday
Invalid input
Friday
Invalid input

solution.approach: The problem is a direct mapping from an integer to the name of a weekday. Store the seven names in an array (or vector) in order Monday to Sunday. For each test case read N, check if it lies between 1 and 7 inclusive. If it does, output the element at index N‑1; otherwise output the literal string "Invalid input". This runs in O(1) per query, so total time is O(T) and only O(1) extra memory is used.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) time overall, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (the array of seven strings is constant).
